commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance

  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

- Added htb-pirate.md (hard/Windows AD — Pre-2K, gMSA, NTLM relay, RBCD, SPN-jacking)
- Restored full SQLi payload anatomy and "Why This Works" breakdown in Gavel

Fix Gradle SIGSEGV crash in Docker builds on ARM (Apple Silicon)

Switch build stage from eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine to eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Cicada, Expressway, Gavel writeups; rewrite DarkZero from actual walkthrough

- Rewrote htb-darkzero.md to match real attack chain (Rubeus, PetitPotam, Golden Ticket)
- Added htb-cicada.md (easy/Windows AD — SMB enum, password spray, SeBackupPrivilege)
- Added htb-expressway.md (easy/Linux — IKE PSK crack, CVE-2025-32463)
- Added htb-gavel.md (medium/Linux — git-dumper, SQLi, PHP rule engine RCE, SUID bash)
- Copied all writeup images to public/images/writeups/{cicada,expressway,gavel}/

Respect no_distill flag when selecting effective content

EffectiveContent() now falls back to original content when:

commit.message:
Fix rolex (#559)

* fix: resolve RoleX v2 plan/todo operations failure

Fixed "No focused plan" error that occurred when executing todo operations
after plan operations. The root cause was that the plan operation wasn't
passing the id parameter through the call chain, preventing focusedPlanId
from being set in the context.

Changes:
- Upgrade all RoleX packages from 1.1.0 to 1.3.0
- Fix RolexBridge.plan() to accept and pass id, after, fallback parameters
- Fix RolexActionDispatcher._plan() to pass all parameters to bridge
- Update MCP tool description with critical warning about plan requiring id
- Add comprehensive examples for complete V2 learning cycle workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update dayu migration workflow for RoleX 1.3.0

Updated dayu (role migration agent) documentation to reflect RoleX 1.3.0
database storage behavior and correct usage patterns based on actual
migration experience.

Key changes:
- Remove outdated "born → activate → synthesize" workflow
- Update to correct pattern: synthesize accepts targetRole parameter directly
- Add critical position naming convention: "角色名+岗位" format required
- Document that appoint's position parameter must match establish's name exactly
- Add verification method: use directory to check members list
- Update MCP tool examples with proper Chinese position names

Based on real migration workflow documented in 流程.txt showing successful
migrations without activate step between born and synthesize operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add v2.2.1 update notification

Added notification for v2.2.1 release highlighting RoleX V2 cognitive cycle
support and Dayu migration fixes.

Changes:
- Add v2.2.1 notification to notification center
- Document core operations: want, plan, todo, finish, achieve, focus, abandon
- Document self-reflection cycle: reflect, realize, master, synthesize, forget
- Highlight Dayu migration feature fix for V1 to V2 role migration
- Add both Chinese and English translations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: guide V2 roles to use action cognitive cycle instead of remember/recall

Updated remember and recall tools to provide helpful guidance when used with
V2 roles, directing users to the appropriate action tool operations instead.

Changes:
- remember tool: Guide V2 roles to use reflect/realize/master/synthesize/forget
- recall tool: Guide V2 roles to use identity/focus for knowledge queries
- Add detailed examples showing correct action tool usage
- Explain that V2 roles use database storage and cognitive cycle system
- Maintain backward compatibility for V1 roles (DPML format)

This improves user experience by providing clear migration paths from V1
memory tools to V2 cognitive cycle operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add V2-only preset questions to AgentX welcome page

Added two new preset questions that only appear when V2 is enabled:
- "Activate Dayu to migrate v1 roles to v2"
- "View current organization structure"

Changes:
- Add dayu and directory preset questions to i18n files (zh-CN and en)
- Update WelcomePage to fetch V2 config on mount
- Conditionally show V2-only questions based on enableV2 setting
- Adjust grid layout: 2 columns (V2 off) or 3 columns (V2 on)
- Add GitBranch and Users icons for new presets

This provides quick access to V2-specific features for users who have
enabled RoleX V2 in system settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-merge new notifications into existing localStorage data

Fixed issue where new notifications wouldn't appear after app restart
because localStorage already contained old notification data.

Changes:
- Modified getNotifications() to check for new default notifications
- Compare existing notification IDs with default notifications
- Auto-merge new notifications to the front of the list
- Preserve existing notifications and their read status

Now when users restart the app, they will see new notifications even if
they already have notification data in localStorage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: complete v2.2.1 changelog with comprehensive release notes

Updated changeset file with detailed release notes covering all changes
in v2.2.1 release.

Highlights:
- RoleX V2 complete cognitive cycle support (want/plan/todo/finish/achieve/focus/abandon)
- Self-reflection cycle (reflect/realize/master/synthesize/forget)
- Fixed plan/todo operations failure (plan requires id parameter)
- Fixed Dayu migration feature for RoleX 1.3.0
- Updated remember/recall tools to guide V2 roles to action tool
- Added V2-only preset questions to AgentX welcome page
- Fixed notification auto-merge issue
- Added position naming convention documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add circadian mode: auto colour temperature by time of day

Firmware: new circadian_mode component with esp_timer (60s periodic),
keyframe-interpolated warm/neutral/cool calculation from wall clock.
BLE char 0xAA10 receives unix epoch from app; ESP32 RTC maintains
time between connections (drifts, re-synced on reconnect).
MODE_FLAG_CIRCADIAN (0x04) stored in scene flags, synced via ESP-NOW.

Flutter: Circadian toggle in mode picker, sliders disabled when active,
CircadianTimerNotifier as app-side fallback, time sync on BLE connect.

Tests: 9 Dart unit tests for CircadianCalculator, ESP-NOW sync test
for circadian flag propagation.

- New AE dashboard with accounts list, health scores, reminders, and activity feed

- Gong integration: sync calls, AI-extracted action items → auto-create reminders
- Google Calendar OAuth flow + event sync to activities
- Gmail/email analysis via Unipile for follow-up reminder suggestions
- Settings tab for connecting Gong (API key) and Google Calendar (OAuth)
- 4 new DB tables: ae_accounts, ae_activities, ae_reminders, ae_integrations (migration 058)
- Feature flags: section_account_executive, ae_gong_integration, ae_calendar_integration, ae_email_analysis
- 5 new edge functions: ae-gong-sync, ae-google-oauth, ae-google-callback, ae-calendar-sync, ae-email-analysis

commit.message:
feat: artist credits traversal + no-dead-ends quality gate

Domain:
- getArtistCredits(): queries release_credits + track_credits, merges by
  release, dedupes roles, classifies role_family (writing/arranging/
  performance/production/other)
- classifyRoleFamily(): deterministic classifier exported from domain

API:
- GET /v1/artists/:id/credits?limit&cursor&role_family
- Additive only — no existing contract changes

Web:
- ArtistCreditsResponse type + isArtistCreditsResponse guard
- Artist page: "Credits & Appearances" section above Releases
- Role-family filter chips (URL-driven, server component)
- No-dead-end: if no masters but credits exist, credits section is first
  actionable list; correct fallback copy per case

DB:
- migration 014: idx_release_credits_artist_batch + idx_track_credits_artist_batch
  (applied directly on Fly internal network — proxy times out on large tables)

Quality gate:
- docs/no-dead-ends-audit-v1.md: per-page dead-end rules + allowed fallback copy
- docs/no-dead-ends-canary-ids.md: 50 fixed IDs including known violations
- scripts/no-dead-ends-check.ts: automated canary check script

commit.message:
feat: smallest-wins resize for multi-client sessions (#72)

Track attached clients per session with their terminal dimensions. When
multiple clients are attached, the PTY is resized to min(cols) x
min(rows) across all clients, recalculated on every attach, detach,
disconnect, and resize event.

The handler now assigns each connection a unique client ID and
registers/unregisters it with the session manager, ensuring cleanup on
all exit paths.

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-06-S11b: FORMULA tr(c_{n-3}) = 2*(n-2)!*(t_3 - C(n,3)/4)

Closed-form formula for the second-highest transfer matrix coefficient:
- tr(c_{n-3}) = 2*(n-2)!*(t_3 - E[t_3]) where E[t_3] = C(n,3)/4
- Measures EXCESS 3-cycle count relative to random tournament
- Verified exactly at n=5 (all classes), n=7 (all classes), n=9 (8 random)
- For regular: per-vertex c_{n-3}[v,v] = (n-1)!/4 universally

configuration, causing all className-based styling to not apply in

anti-aliasing for crisp, non-blurry rendering at all icon sizes.

commit.message:
fix(onboarding): re-apply celebration modal race condition fix

Replit's MagicLoader/ShinyText commits re-introduced the auth gate
race condition: refreshSession() in handleComplete triggers useAuthGate
which unmounts onboarding before celebration modal renders.

Moved refreshSession() to handleEnterAspire (fires on "Enter Aspire"
click) so the auth gate stays dormant during loading + celebration.
